steps.title: Set the function equal to zero
steps.content:
An $x$-intercept occurs where $y=0$, so set $f(x)$ equal to $0$:
$$
(x+6)(x-4)=0
$$

steps.title: Use the zero product rule
steps.content: A product equals $0$ when at least one factor equals $0$. Since the question asks for an $x$-coordinate of an intercept, one valid coordinate comes from the first factor. The answer is -6.
